mission.title: opentelemetry: SIG documents should be owned by an OpenTelemetry account
mission.description: SIG documents should be owned by an OpenTelemetry account. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current opentelemetry deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentelemetry version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry
helm list -n opentelemetry 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentelemetry installation.

steps.title: Review opentelemetry configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry
```
Related #1146

All SIG meeting note documents should be owned by an OpenTelemetry or CNCF google account. This will prevent access issues like we have seen before in the related issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for SIG documents should be owned by an OpenTelemetry account
steps.description:
Related #1147 
Logs doc previously owned by  recreated because it could not be transferred

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "SIG documents should be owned by an OpenTelemetry account" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Related #1147 
Logs doc previously owned by  recreated because it could not be transferred

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry installed or the issue environment reproducible.
